finding: Two orthogonal evaluative dimensions (valence and dominance) are sufficient to describe rapid automatic judgments; one dimension reflects inferential features signaling approach/avoidance while the other reflects features signaling capability.

A surface-level property (concreteness) of identical propositional content increases perceived truthfulness; this effect emerges especially when cognitive framing aligns with the property (fit effect).

A physiological trait (threat reactivity) predicts the direction of evaluative preferences toward external and internal social order; lower reactivity associates with preferences for openness while higher reactivity associates with preferences for stability.
Availability of cognitive elaboration time and absence of post-hoc rationalization suppress a default self-serving response; honesty emerges when deliberation replaces intuitive decision-making.
